ambiguities.description: What entities, relationships, and boundaries define this system?
How do users and processes interact with the system over time?
What rules, constraints, and compliance requirements apply?
What external systems, integrations, and deployment constraints exist?
What defines MVP, done, and out-of-scope for this construction?
Trigger sources (event, schedule, webhook, manual, external system)
Action targets (API, database, notification, external system)
State and retry semantics (exactly-once, at-least-once, at-most-once)
Approval requirements before destructive or sensitive actions
Auditability requirements (who triggered what, when, and outcome)
Failure handling (retry, dead letter, alert, manual recovery)
External integration constraints (rate limits, auth, availability)
Scheduling and time semantics (timezone, daylight saving, cron)

questions.question: What does success look like for this system?
Who are the primary users and what is the organization size?
What is the timeline and what constraints (budget, compliance, team) apply?
What are the primary trigger sources?
What are the primary action targets?
Which actions require human approval before execution?
How should failures be handled (retry, dead letter, alert)?

assumptions.assumption: Workflows are primarily automated with optional human approval gates
Standard retry with exponential backoff is sufficient for transient failures

suggested_closures.decision: Workflow construction will separate trigger detection, state management, and action execution into distinct layers.
suggested_closures.rationale: Separation enables independent evolution of triggers and actions, and supports retry without re-execution.

seed_tasks.title: Define system scope and success criteria
seed_tasks.transformation: Document what the system must do, for whom, and what success looks like.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: A written scope document exists and has been reviewed.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: A third party can understand the scope without asking clarifying questions.

seed_tasks.title: Identify constraints and non-goals
seed_tasks.transformation: Explicitly list budget, timeline, compliance, and out-of-scope items.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: No hidden constraints remain that would invalidate the construction plan.

seed_tasks.title: Map trigger sources and event schema
seed_tasks.transformation: Document all trigger sources, their event shapes, and validation rules.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Trigger inventory and event schema exist.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Every trigger has a defined schema and source.

seed_tasks.title: Define action targets and integration contracts
seed_tasks.transformation: Document action targets, their APIs, auth, and failure modes.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Each action has defined success/failure criteria.

seed_tasks.title: Design state and retry semantics
seed_tasks.transformation: Choose and document delivery guarantee, state machine, and retry policy.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: State machine and retry policy are documented.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: A developer can implement the workflow engine from the document.

residuals.description: System scope and success criteria are not yet defined by the principal.
Constraints, compliance requirements, and non-goals have not been documented.
Delivery guarantee (exactly-once, etc.) is not selected.
